title: Emergency warning signs
content: Chest pain, difficulty breathing, stroke-like symptoms, severe bleeding, fainting, seizures, blue lips, or sudden confusion should be treated as emergency warning signs requiring immediate medical care.

title: Fever and infection guidance
content: High fever, persistent fever, fever with stiff neck, dehydration, confusion, chest pain, or breathing difficulty needs prompt clinical assessment. Mild fever can often be monitored with hydration and rest.

title: Respiratory symptom reference
content: Cough with shortness of breath, wheezing, chest pain, blue lips, or worsening symptoms may indicate higher respiratory risk and should be evaluated by a healthcare professional.

title: Vomiting and dehydration reference
content: Persistent vomiting, inability to keep fluids down, blood in vomit or stool, severe abdominal pain, or signs of dehydration require urgent medical assessment.

title: Medical history risk factors
content: People with diabetes, heart disease, pregnancy, asthma, COPD, immune suppression, or other chronic conditions may need earlier clinical evaluation when symptoms are moderate or worsening.

title: Supportive home care
content: Mild cold symptoms, minor headache, or low-severity symptoms without warning signs can often be managed with rest, hydration, and monitoring. Professional care is advised if symptoms persist or worsen.
